terms.definition: Representation of a categorical variable as binary indicator columns—exactly one column is 1 per row for that feature (per category level), avoiding implied order between levels.
Mapping ordered category levels to integers that preserve rank (e.g. small=0, medium=1, large=2) so models can treat stepwise increases consistently.
Replacing a category with a statistic of the target (mean, rate) computed from training rows in that category; also called mean encoding when the statistic is the mean.
Assigning an arbitrary integer to each category label; convenient for tree libraries but misleading for linear models unless levels are truly ordinal.
The number of distinct category levels in a feature; high cardinality pushes toward target encoding, hashing, or grouping instead of full one-hot expansion.
A label present at inference but absent from the training vocabulary; encoders must define ignore, unknown-bucket, or fallback behavior.
